verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a single-pass (type 1) transmembrane protein resident in the ER membrane whose cytoplasmic C-terminus translocates to ER-PM junctions upon Ca2+ store depletion, where it directly gates ORAI channels. Crucially, a fraction of STIM1 is also constitutively present at the plasma membrane with its N-terminal EF-hand domain exposed to the extracellular space. This ectodomain is accessible from the outer leaflet and has been targeted by extracellular-binding reagents. As a type-I TM protein with a documented extracellular N-terminal domain at the PM, the protein body satisfies the surface-accessibility criterion by its own membrane anchor.
